parsed_review.summary: CP00-439 is a descriptor-only settlement-core slice (RP14.P05.M04.S08-RP14.P05.M06.S03, risk B, 40 units) and passes independent read-only review with no findings. The pack-level claim is correctly scoped: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, asserting only descriptor verification (settlement_core_p05_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor_verified), not runtime or enterprise-trust readiness — so no production_ready gate is weakened. Changed-file scope matches active_pack_implementation_refs (contract JSON, README, registry/service/validators, model.test.js, validator script); unrelated dirty/untracked files are correctly excluded. CP439 additions in registry.js (pack binding, no-write attestation, requirements), service.js:1675-1791 (freezeCp439Result + case-set/descriptor) and validators.js:2388-2555 (coverage/slice/hermes/claude/handoff) faithfully follow the established CP426->CP438 pattern, with all runtime-dispatch/write/leak flags set false. Deliverable counts reconcile exactly (impl 16, security_audit 4, fixture 11, test 6, hermes_evidence 2, claude_review 1 = 40) and micro rows (M04:15, M05:22, M06:3 = 40). All seven required public exports exist and are re-exported via index.js; model.test.js:917-967 covers coverage, descriptor-only rows, authority boundaries, evidence packets, handoff, and no-write attestation. Cross-artifact consistency holds: the validator asserts the committed contract deep-equals live code output and requires CP00-439 in the live closeout-pack-plan.json, which carries CP00-439 as the current first planned pack with deviation_from_plan=false; README#cp00-439 is accurate. Review-hardening boundaries are intact: Claude is pinned read-only and not final approver, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, forbidden_review_evidence rejects auth-failure/zero-byte/malformed-JSON/fenced-JSON/tool-call-shaped/missing-field evidence, the readiness auth preflight passed, and no invalid attempts are recorded. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packets/receipts (descriptor_only...no_write), not product runtime (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). No stubs, TODOs, or placeholders in changed source. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are expected later in the hardened sequence and are appropriately absent now. Final human approval for any enterprise-trust claim remains outside this receipt.
